phase.automation-note: bash tools/cycle-runner close-out --issue {N} Auto-posts steps C4.1, C4.5, C5, C5.1, C5.5, C5.6, C6, C7, C8. You handle: C1, C2, C3 manually before running cycle-runner close-out. Prerequisites: Run cycle-complete, write-entry worklog, and write-entry journal. The C3 worklog is a preliminary pre-dispatch snapshot until cycle-runner freezes it from the recorded C5.5 gate state during C5. The tool is idempotent. If gate fails, fix and re-run.

step.title: Pipeline verification (early)
step.description: Early check before state modifications. Not the final gate.

step.title: Update state.json via tools

at-cycle-end.constraint: Run cycle-complete BEFORE dispatching review agent.

step.note: When consuming a review via process-review, pass the review dispatch issue explicitly with --review-issue N so the resulting review_agent.history[].review_issue field preserves the review linkage.

step.title: Draft documentation entries
step.constraint: The pipeline status written here is PRELIMINARY. The worklog must render the pre-dispatch snapshot note and MUST NOT be treated as the published/final artifact yet. Cycle-runner freezes the worklog from docs/state.json::tool_pipeline.c5_5_gate during C5, re-runs validate-docs, and only then commits/pushes the published cycle artifact. Recurrence escalation: process-adherence in cycles 420-421 and chronic worklog-accuracy through cycle 453.

When ANY blocking gate (C4.1 or C5.5) fails during a cycle, the worklog pipeline status MUST record the initial failure, even if a re-run later succeeds. Format: "Pipeline status: FAIL→PASS (C5.5 initially failed: [reason]; resolved by [action])". The journal "What fell short" section MUST center the gate failure as the primary shortcoming, not bury it. Commitments referencing C5.5 outcomes MUST NOT be graded as "Followed" until C5.5 has actually been run and passed — grading against the C1 early check is insufficient. Recurrence escalation: all three categories in cycles 442-443.

When a C5.5 FAIL is resolved by modifying the gate exclusion list or gate criteria (rather than fixing the underlying condition), the worklog MUST record this as a "gate-criteria change" or "gate override," NOT as a "re-run" or "resolved by re-run." The specific exclusions added and the architectural rationale (e.g., ADR reference) MUST be stated. The journal "What fell short" section MUST acknowledge the gate-criteria change as a process deviation requiring architectural justification. Recurrence escalation: process-adherence in cycle 482 (F2).

Journal commitments MUST reference checks and conditions that exist today and are directly observable at grading time. Each commitment specifies: (1) the exact command or artifact to check, (2) the expected outcome. Grade against stated observable condition — do not defer a commitment whose condition is already checkable. Do not reference pipeline phases or tool outputs that do not yet exist. Recurrence escalation: journal-quality in cycles 420-421.

Commitment follow-through grading MUST verify the named observable survived the LIVE cycle path, not just that the implementing PR merged. "Merged" ≠ "worked." If a same-cycle hotfix was required to make the merged implementation actually pass in the live close-out, record the commitment as "met-after-fix" or "partially met" with an explanation, NOT as a clean success. Grading a commitment as fully met when its implementation failed on the live path and required a follow-up fix is dishonest and will be flagged. Recurrence escalation: journal-quality in cycle 481 (F2).

When writing the "Concrete commitments for next cycle" section, any commitment that has appeared on the list for 2+ prior cycles WITHOUT action (tracked via previous-commitment follow-through section) MUST be handled in one of two ways: (a) ACTIONED or dispatched this cycle. Drop from the commitments list and cite the action/dispatch in "What was done". (b) DEMOTED out of the commitments list into a separate "Backlog (capacity-blocked)" section that follows the commitments section. Each demoted item must carry a one-line rationale explaining why it cannot be actioned (e.g., "dispatch-required, all slots full"). Once demoted, an item must NOT be re-promoted to commitments without new action on it. The journal MUST NOT contain a "commitments for next cycle" list that names an item while the same journal's prose acknowledges the recurrence-escalation rule requires action-or-drop. That is a self-aware rule violation and will be flagged by the review agent. Recurrence escalation: journal-quality (chronic) — cycle 476 F3 caught the cycle 476 journal doing exactly this: it quoted the STARTUP_CHECKLIST.xml recurrence rule ("must action this cycle or drop with rationale") and then said "I am choosing to carry rather than drop" for commitments 4-7. This constraint makes the rule procedurally enforceable in the checklist, not just a norm cited in prose and then overridden.

Journal decision summaries MUST reflect the FINAL disposition state, not the initial S0.5 assessment. If review finding dispositions change during the cycle (e.g., a deferred finding is later actioned or dispatched), the journal must report the final counts. Verify against state.json review_agent.history before writing. Recurrence escalation: journal-quality in cycle 430.

The commit receipts table in the worklog MUST be strictly machine-scoped to cycle-receipts output. Never append manual "direct-edit" rows or post-cycle-complete commits to the auto-generated table. If a post-cycle corrective commit needs to be documented (e.g., in_flight_sessions reconcile, state counter sync), record it in a separate narrative section BELOW the table (e.g., "Post-cycle-complete corrections") so the table's stated cutoff time remains honest. Mixing manual entries into the auto-generated table corrupts receipt-integrity: a reader cannot trust the --through timestamp if rows landed after it. Recurrence escalation: receipt-integrity (chronic) — cycle 475 F1 re-flagged the same pattern one cycle after cycle 474 cited receipt drift. Structural tool fix (cycle-receipts/receipt-validate warning on mixed-source rows) remains queued.

The worklog "Issues processed" section MUST list only issues on which the cycle took a concrete cycle-scoped action: a new comment posted this cycle, a PR opened or merged against the issue this cycle, the issue closed or re-labeled this cycle, or a dispatch tied to the issue number in the current cycle window. Do NOT list issues that are only referenced in prose as: - deferred-target dependencies (e.g., "F2 already dispatched to #2293") - structural blockers cited as context - commitment carryovers from prior cycles without new activity - named examples in narrative explanation The auto-issues derive function (write-entry --auto-issues) scans what_was_done for #NNN references and is known to over-include. After running it, review the rendered "Issues processed" list and manually drop any entries that lack a matching cycle-scoped receipt (comment, merge, dispatch, close, label). An issue referenced only as the home for a deferred structural response is NOT a processed issue. Recurrence escalation: worklog-accuracy (chronic) — cycle 476 F1 flagged #2293 listed as "processed" when it was only cited as the structural home for a deferred journal-quality response. Matches the cycle 474/475 receipt-integrity pattern of auto-derivation over- inclusion. Structural tool fix (derive_issue_processed_entries filter on cycle-scope receipts) remains queued; this procedural constraint is the in-cycle backstop.

step.title: Validate documentation (BLOCKING GATE)
step.on-failure: Fix the issue. Re-run validation. Do NOT proceed to C5.

step.title: ADR check
step.description: Check if decisions this cycle warrant ADRs. Runs after C4.1 validation.

adr-worthy.criterion: New architectural patterns or significant refactors

step.constraint: Must be posted before C5.5 gate runs.

step.title: Freeze docs from C5.5 and push
step.constraint: Worklog, journal, and state.json MUST be committed and pushed BEFORE review dispatch (C6). The review agent reads the repo at dispatch time. Uncommitted docs = false positive "missing" findings.

The published worklog and journal files MUST NOT be mutated after C5.5 passes. The artifact validated at the gate is the artifact that gets reviewed. Adding "(post-dispatch)" sections, refreshing in-flight counts, appending new "Issues processed" entries, or any other content change after C5.5 is PROHIBITED. Anything that happens post-dispatch belongs in the NEXT cycle's worklog, not as an amendment to the already-validated published cycle. The only permitted exception is a one-time corrective revert in a later cycle when the published file fails doc-validation (which itself must include a clear corrective note explaining the edit). Routine "post-dispatch refresh" is forbidden. Recurrence escalation: worklog-accuracy + process-adherence + journal-quality in cycle 453 (cycle 453 review F1+F2+F3 — all three findings shared the same root cause: post-dispatch worklog refresh that broke doc-validation).

sequence.action: Re-run validate-docs on the frozen worklog/journal and abort if it fails.
Commit all worklog and journal entries.
Push docs + state commits to master and verify success before proceeding.

scope-exclusions.reason: Structurally created after worklog is written

step.title: Final pipeline gate (BLOCKING GATE)
step.constraint: All 5 phases MUST pass before review dispatch.
step.on-failure: Fix before closing cycle. Do not override.
step.coordination-rule: When deploying format-changing tools: merge downstream consumer update BEFORE applying new tool output.
step.constraint: When C5.5 is re-run after a mid-cycle fix and the new pipeline status differs from the previously-posted C5.5 step comment, a NEW C5.5 step comment titled "Step C5.5 (rerun, supersedes prior)" MUST be posted to the orchestrator issue. This ensures the issue thread reflects the FINAL gate state, not the pre-fix failure narrative. The cycle-runner close-out automation may post this; the orchestrator must verify the comment is present after a rerun before continuing to C6. Recurrence escalation: cycle 449 F3 process-adherence (deferred 5 cycles, deadline cycle 454). Behavioral fix in lieu of tool dispatch — when the audit #382 worklog freeze-ordering fix lands, C5.5 reruns should become much rarer, but the discipline remains required.

If C5.5 fails, you may fix the underlying issue and re-run close-out. However, re-dispositioning prior-cycle review findings specifically to clear a blocking gate (e.g., changing "deferred" to "actioned" on a prior review to pass mass-deferral-gate) is PROHIBITED. Prior-cycle dispositions are frozen once the cycle that set them has completed. Any disposition change to a prior cycle requires a process-review --note explaining why the original disposition was incorrect, separate from gate-clearing motivation. Recurrence escalation: process-adherence + worklog-accuracy in cycles 442-443.

when.action: Increment clean_cycle_counter. Append cycle to consecutive_clean_cycles.
Reset clean_cycle_counter to 0. Clear array. Log reason in journal.

substep.title: Copilot availability gate
substep.action: Skip dispatch. Post step comment noting deferral.

review-spec.adversarial-mandate: Your job is to find everything wrong with this cycle's work. Be thorough. Be skeptical. If something looks fine on the surface, dig deeper.

review-targets.target: Merged PRs, direct pushes. Quality, tests, conventions.
Cross-reference claims against commits, state.json, issue activity.
Genuine reflection or boilerplate? Observable commitments?
Metrics current? Freshness accurate? Run spot-checks.
Verify hashes. Receipt scope: through cycle-complete. Docs commit and record-dispatch commit STRUCTURALLY EXCLUDED -- do NOT flag their absence.
AGENTS.md, skills, checklists consistent with practice?
Followed checklists? Used tools? Check temporal ordering of step comments (created_at timestamps).
Going through motions? Findings noted but not fixed? Deferrals accumulating?

output-format.template:
## N. [category-name] Finding title
**File**: path/to/file:line **Evidence**: what was observed **Recommendation**: concrete action
output-format.rule: Category tag MUST appear in heading inside square brackets.
output-format.scoring-cap: If cycle overrode any FAIL/blocking pipeline gate, max score = 3/5.
output-format.depth-preference: Three deeply investigated findings with evidence beat ten surface observations.

review-spec.stabilization-overlay: OBSERVATION MODE (ADR 0011): Log all findings in standard format. Do NOT classify any as requiring immediate action. All findings logged for post-stabilization triage. Your role is forensic documentation.

dispatch-mechanics.note: The tool handles issue creation, labels (agent-task, cycle-review), model selection, and agent assignment. Write the review body to a temp file first, then pass it via --body-file. Use --dry-run to preview.
dispatch-mechanics.constraint: Agent must commit findings as docs/reviews/cycle-NNN.md (agents cannot post issue comments).

substep.title: chronic-refresh-invalidation
substep.description: After this cycle's review is consumed, run the warning-only pipeline-check chronic-refresh-invalidation step to detect chronic category refreshes from the reviewed cycle that were invalidated by that same cycle's review categories.
substep.action: If flagged, manually run process-review --rollback-chronic-category CATEGORY:PRIOR_VC:RATIONALE for each listed category before treating the chronic response as current.
substep.constraint: Tier 1 backstop: do not leave same-cycle fabricated freshness markers in review_agent.chronic_category_responses after adversarial review consumption.

step.title: Fallback self-review
step.condition: C6 dispatch failed AND previous also failed

checklist.item: Receipt verification: run cycle-receipts, verify all.
Step comment audit: verify all mandatory steps posted.
State.json integrity: run pipeline-check, confirm invariants.
Worklog accuracy: cross-reference claims against git history.

required-sections.section: Header: Self-review -- independent review agent unavailable
Summary: N findings (A actioned, D deferred, I ignored)

step.escalation: File question-for-eva after 2 consecutive dispatch failures.

step.title: Commit dispatch state and push
step.action: record-dispatch already committed state change. Push immediately.
step.constraint: This receipt appears in closing comment but NOT in worklog receipt table.
